chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: Participants take turns sharing their individual research backgrounds and the specific challenges they face, expanding the scope of the discussion without yet attempting to converge on a single shared project.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Non-speaking participants, particularly Michelle and Alexis, display visible head nods and attentive facial expressions while others are speaking, though the speaker-view recording limits full visibility.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Connecting biological models to physical tools.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group has identified a shared domain of interest—understanding cell behavior in complex, multicellular environments—but has not formulated a specific research question.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The discussion consists of open exploration of individual interests and challenges, with no specific project idea proposed or agreed upon.
chunk_summary.complementarity_recognition_quote: interested to hear from the physical tool people about how we can get more out of these systems
chunk_summary.skill_gap_description: Michelle notes the difficulty of extracting data from complex 3D biological models and asks physical tool experts for solutions.

ideas_currently_on_table: Measuring protein diffusion in tissues vs isolated cells
Balancing holistic tissue info with molecular info
Chemical tools to track dynamics in 3D multicellular models
Using synthetic ECMs to decouple cell-environment factors
chunk_summaries: The session began with Gang-yu Liu facilitating a structured round-robin introduction where each participant shared their affiliation and a brief summary of their research. The group then assigned the roles of reporter and scribe to Michelle and Anna, respectively. The chunk ended with Gang-yu and Paul instructing the participants to take a minute to write down important research questions to kick off the scientific discussion.

The group transitioned into scientific discussion, with participants taking turns sharing their research backgrounds and the specific challenges they face. Paul Selvin raised a question about measuring protein movement in tissues versus isolated cells, prompting Alexis Komor, Michelle Farkas, and Ronit Freeman to share their perspectives on balancing holistic and molecular information in complex biological models. The tone was collaborative and exploratory, with Michelle explicitly asking for input from physical tool experts to help address biological complexity. The chunk ended in divergent exploration with several related ideas on the table but no specific joint project identified.
